commit.message:
Inject COOP/COEP headers via service worker for GH Pages

GitHub Pages doesn't support custom headers. The service worker
now adds Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy and Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy
to navigation responses, enabling SharedArrayBuffer for ffmpeg.wasm.
On first visit, the page reloads once after the SW takes control.

fix: set status_id=NULL when MySQL status=0 in is_pedidos_historico

is_extras_status has IDs 1-35 only (no id=0). MySQL uses status=0 as
a sentinel meaning "no status". Map it to NULL (column is nullable).

commit.message:
fix: add PnP token bridge for SPO connection on Linux/macOS

Connect-SPOService fails with 400 Bad Request on Linux due to the SPO
module using Windows-specific auth flows. Fall back to PnP.PowerShell
which handles modern auth cross-platform, grab its SPO access token,
and pass it to Connect-SPOService -AccessToken.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: flag 2012-awarded-not-delegated strings (RES-009)

Adds a new data file prior-round.ts tracking TLD strings that were
awarded in the ICANN 2012 round but have never been delegated due to
ongoing legal proceedings or unresolved contention.

.web is the primary case: awarded to Nu Dot Co LLC at auction for
$135M in July 2016, still trapped in ICANN IRP and Verisign litigation
as of 2026. A new application would be rejected — ICANN would not
accept it while the prior-round award is unresolved.

checkReserved now raises flag RES-009 (HIGH severity) for these strings,
triggering isHardBlocked = true and both scores = 100.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Apollo search: limit q_keywords to first 2 to avoid zero results

Apollo treats q_keywords as a single search query. Joining all ICP profile
keywords into one long string (e.g. "AI strategy machine learning data-driven
decision making digital transformation innovation management") returns 0
results. Limiting to first 2 keywords keeps the search broad enough while
the other filters (titles, seniorities, industries, etc.) provide targeting.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix OpenSSL dependency issue by using rustls instead of native-tls

Replace reqwest's default native-tls with rustls-tls to eliminate
system OpenSSL dependency, fixing CI build failures.

Changes:
- Use rustls-tls instead of native-tls for reqwest
- Disable default features to avoid native-tls
- Add explicit rustls-tls feature

Benefits:
- No system OpenSSL dependency required
- Pure Rust TLS implementation
- Easier CI/CD builds
- Cross-platform compatibility

This fixes the build error:
  error: failed to run custom build command for `openssl-sys v0.9.111`
  Could not find directory of OpenSSL installation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Musical control layer (freeze, morph, harmonic filter, responsiveness) (#207)

Implement Milestone 4 (Phases 13-14) for Innexus: four new expressive
parameters for real-time harmonic manipulation.

- Harmonic Freeze: manual toggle captures HarmonicFrame/ResidualFrame,
  ≤10ms crossfade on disengage, overrides auto-freeze
- Morph Position: 0-1 blend between frozen and live states with 7ms
  smoother, per-partial amplitude/frequency/residual interpolation
- Harmonic Filter Type: 5 presets (All-Pass, Odd/Even Only, Low/High
  Harmonics) as per-partial amplitude masks after morph pipeline
- Responsiveness: forwards to HarmonicModelBuilder dual-timescale blend,
  default 0.5 matches M1/M3 behavior exactly
- HarmonicFrameUtils (KrateDSP Layer 2): lerpHarmonicFrame,
  lerpResidualFrame, computeHarmonicMask, applyHarmonicMask
- State version 4 with v1/v2/v3 backward compatibility
- 158 innexus tests, 6250 dsp tests all passing
- Pluginval strictness 5 pass

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add Capital One Distinguished Engineer role and create about.md

Added Capital One Card Tech Distinguished Engineer to overview and
companies sections in both index.html and the new about.md markdown page.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Priority scheduling, resource limits, shell scripting, TCP retransmission, and conditional debug prints

Kernel:
- 4-level priority MLQ scheduler (realtime/high/normal/low) with work stealing
- Per-process resource limits (CPU ticks, memory pages) enforced in scheduler and frame_alloc
- Enhanced panic handler: recursive prevention, thread info, RBP stack trace, NMI halt broadcast
- New syscalls: ThreadInfo(140), ResourceLimit(141), ThreadCount(142)
- Conditional debug output: kdebug! macro gated behind 'verbose' feature flag (on by default)

Shell (LUCAS v0.2):
- Pipe operator (cmd1 | cmd2) with buffer-based capture
- Background execution (command &) via clone
- Environment variables (export KEY=VAL, $VAR expansion, env builtin)
- Shell scripting: if/then/fi conditionals, for/in/do/done loops
- New builtins: head, tail, grep, top, env

Drivers:
- NVMe: PRP List support for multi-page transfers, IPC service protocol (READ/WRITE/INFO)
- NVMe service registered as 'nvme' via svc_register for block I/O requests

Networking:
- TCP retransmission with exponential backoff (saved segments, max 5 retries, auto-reset on timeout)
- Retransmit state cleared on ACK, tracked per-connection

Filesystem:
- File permissions model: permissions/owner_uid/owner_gid fields on DirEntry
- Default permissions set on create (0o644 files, 0o755 dirs)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: sotOS
repository.description: A microkernel operating system written from scratch in Rust, designed around five kernel primitives: scheduling, IRQ virtualization, IPC, capabilities, and frame allocation. Virtual memory is managed entirely in userspace.  sotOS stands for Secure Object Transactional Operating System — reflecting its capability-based security model, object-oriente

commit.message:
Add db-sanity-check binary for local DB validation (#2013)

* first draft of db_sanity_check

* adjustments and docs

* Compact db-sanity-check: 1117 → 870 lines

- Unify CheckResult constructors into single `new(id, name, ok, detail)`
- Merge 7 separate per-eye graph check loops into one
- Use iterators (flat_map/filter/count) for orphan, hierarchy, neighbor,
  self-loop checks instead of explicit loops with counters
- Use custom serde serializer for status field instead of manual JSON building
- Pass checks/stats as &mut instead of returning and extending
- Simplify Stats to a newtype wrapper

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refinements

* Drop iris integrity checks, use SQL for ID collection and cross-schema comparison

- Remove checks 1a-1d (iris table integrity) — redundant given the
  GPU cross-schema comparison treats GPU as source of truth
- Replace stream_irises() with SELECT id for iris ID collection
  (8 bytes/row instead of ~77KB/row)
- Replace streaming byte comparison with SQL JOIN between schemas
  (comparison happens in Postgres, only mismatched IDs cross the wire)
- Remove persistent_state dump from stats
- Renumber checks: 1=graph, 2=persistent state, 3=cross-schema, 4=mods

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* scope cross-schema checks to last_indexed_id; drop modifications check

Cross-schema iris comparisons (3a-3c) now use WHERE id <= last_indexed_id
since GPU table can be larger than HNSW. Removed modifications table check
(4a) — the HNSW modifications table is cleared after genesis, and
modification correctness is already captured by the byte-identical iris
comparison (3c).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* soften cross-schema iris check

* add duplicate neighbors, geometric layer density checks; log last_indexed_modification_id

- 1f: check no duplicate entries in neighbor lists
- 1j: check layer node count ratios approximate geometric distribution
  with parameter q (--layer-probability, default 1/M), 3x tolerance
- Stats: log last_indexed_modification_id from persistent_state
- 3c: make cross-schema byte comparison informational (always passes)
  since modifications can land between genesis and this check

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* use Binomial(N, q^L) model for layer density check

Instead of ratio-based tolerance, compute expected node count per layer
from Binomial(N, q^L) and flag layers more than 3 standard deviations
from expected. Naturally tightens for large layers, loosens for small.

* cargo fmt

* adjustments

* separate eye checks completely to reduce peak memory

* cargo fmt

* redo docs

* add sampling for cross-schema validation

* adjust

* cargo fmt

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs(db-sanity-check): add --seed arg, update checks 2a and 3c descriptions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(db-sanity-check): decouple check 2a from 2b, drop wasteful ORDER BY

- Check 2a now only tests `last_indexed_iris_id == irises max serial ID`;
  the `left_max != right_max` condition was removed since that belongs
  exclusively to check 2b, preventing double-reporting the same failure.
- Drop `ORDER BY id` in collect_iris_ids — results go into a HashSet so
  ordering was irrelevant; use .max() to compute the stat instead.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* adjustments & S3 outputs

* feat(db-sanity-check): decouple DB URLs, add S3 output & report.txt

- Replace single --db-url with --hnsw-db-url / --gpu-db-url for
  separate HNSW and GPU Postgres instances (production layout)
- Cross-schema checks now use separate queries + in-memory comparison
  instead of SQL JOINs across schemas
- Add --s3-output <S3_URI> for optional upload of JSON + report files
- Add Report struct: tees human-readable output to stdout and report.txt
- Detect one-sided iris IDs (present in one DB but not the other) as
  warnings rather than mismatches
- Update DB_SANITY_CHECK.md docs for new CLI args and output files
- Add 10s sleep in genesis e2e script before sanity checks

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(db-sanity-check): relax 3a/3b to informational logs

Row count and max serial ID comparisons between HNSW and GPU schemas
are now logged as informational output rather than hard PASS/FAIL
checks, since mismatches here are expected during normal operation.

commit.message:
fix: use @ separator in tmux session names instead of : (#53)

* fix: use @ separator in tmux session names instead of :

tmux interprets ':' in -t target strings as a session:window separator,
so a session named "repo:branch" would cause switch-client to look for
window "branch" inside session "repo" rather than the full session name.

Change the separator to '@' which has no special meaning in tmux targets
while still distinguishing between overlapping repo/branch name combos.

Fixes #52

Co-authored-by: Eli Oshinsky <eli0shin@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove portfolio weight scaling from live position sizing

instead of being scaled down by portfolio weight (10-50%). Portfolio
weight is only used for backtest WF return aggregation.

Also increase max_open_positions 3 → 4 to match component count.

fix: PWA auto-updates — network-first HTML + skipWaiting

- sw.js: network-first for navigation (index.html always fresh from server)
  cache-first for hashed assets (JS/CSS) — safe since Vite content-hashes them
- skipWaiting() so new SW activates immediately without waiting for tab close
- clients.claim() takes control of all open tabs right away
- Old caches (v1, v2) deleted on activation
- main.jsx: reload page automatically when new SW takes control

Users will now get updates on next page load instead of being stuck
on old cached versions indefinitely.

commit.message:
fix(clemens): promote CONDITIONAL+HIGH rule to top-level critical rule

The CONDITIONAL + HIGH auto-fix logic was embedded in the per-slice
loop pseudo-code, but the orchestrator parallelizes wave reviews and
makes batch decisions, bypassing the per-slice loop. By promoting
it to rule #6 in KRITISCHE REGELN, the LLM sees it before any
execution logic and applies it even when parallelizing reviews.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix gvisor read stall: remove Connection: close, add per-read deadlines

DisableKeepAlives causes http.Transport to send "Connection: close",
which makes ClickHouse FIN the TCP connection after responding. gvisor's
userspace TCP stalls reading buffered data after receiving FIN — small
responses complete before FIN arrives, but 35KB spans multiple segments
and hits the stall.

Fix: Remove DisableKeepAlives (no more Connection: close), wrap tsnet
connections with deadlineConn that refreshes read deadline on every
Read() call, and aggressively close idle connections after each request.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
